sources.title: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
WP Maps Pro bug exploited to create admin accounts on WordPress sites
Security Affairs newsletter Round 579 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
27,000-Download Codex UI Tool Secretly Stole OpenAI Refresh Tokens
CVE-2026-0257: Rapid7 Caught Attackers Abusing Forged VPN Cookies Against Multiple Customers

executive_brief: This week’s threat landscape reveals a systematic dismantling of enterprise trust boundaries. Attackers have abandoned opportunistic scanning in favor of poisoning update pipelines, forging perimeter credentials, and hijacking web infrastructure at scale. The Dutch authorities’ dismantling of a 17-million-device botnet [1] exposes the massive infrastructure backing these campaigns, while simultaneous exploitation of FortiClient EMS [3, 4], Ghost CMS [3, 4], and Palo Alto GlobalProtect [6] demonstrates a deliberate strategy to bypass both endpoint and network defenses. Security leaders must recognize that authentication mechanisms, vendor update channels, and web platforms are no longer safe defaults. The convergence of supply chain poisoning, mass web compromise, and cryptographic bypass requires immediate remediation and a fundamental shift toward zero-trust validation of all inbound updates and outbound connections.

threat_stories.headline: The Poisoned Pipeline: Enterprise Update Mechanisms Weaponized
threat_stories.narrative:
Threat actors have turned a critical vulnerability in FortiClient EMS into a delivery mechanism for the EKZ Infostealer, exploiting the inherent trust organizations place in vendor update channels [3, 4]. By disguising malicious payloads as legitimate Fortinet patches, attackers bypass security controls and achieve persistence without triggering traditional signature-based detection. The exploitation requires minimal user interaction, leveraging the EMS update process to silently deploy credential-stealing malware across managed endpoints [3, 4]. This campaign represents a direct attack on the enterprise security stack itself, proving that endpoint management platforms can be weaponized against their own users. Security teams must assume that automatic update channels are compromised until cryptographic verification is enforced at the network boundary.

The sophistication of this campaign extends beyond simple malware distribution. Attackers map EMS deployment architectures, identify update endpoints, and inject spoofed patch files that carry valid digital signatures or bypass verification routines entirely [3, 4]. Once executed, EKZ Infostealer harvests browser credentials, session tokens, and enterprise authentication data, granting threat actors lateral movement capabilities. This is not a theoretical risk; exploitation is active and widespread across environments relying on automated EMS patching.

Organizations that treat vendor updates as inherently safe are operating under a dangerous assumption. The FortiClient EMS campaign proves that supply chain trust must be validated cryptographically at every hop. Security operations centers must treat update channels as hostile vectors until proven otherwise, implementing strict signature verification and network-level isolation for management traffic.

timeline.event: Attackers begin targeting FortiClient EMS update endpoints across enterprise networks
timeline.significance: Initial deployment of spoofed patch files containing EKZ Infostealer payloads

timeline.event: Credential harvesting and lateral movement observed across multiple victim environments
timeline.significance: Confirms active exploitation and successful persistence establishment

timeline.event: Public disclosure of CVE-2026-35616 and active exploitation campaigns
timeline.significance: Forces immediate remediation and exposes scale of supply chain poisoning

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Organizations relying on FortiClient EMS for endpoint management face immediate credential theft, session hijacking, and lateral movement risks. Automated update pipelines are actively compromised.
threat_stories.action_required: Disable automatic EMS updates immediately. Verify all patch checksums and digital signatures against Fortinet’s official repository before deployment. Isolate affected endpoints, reset all credentials accessed through compromised systems, and implement network-level validation for EMS traffic [3, 4].

threat_stories.headline: Web Infrastructure Hijacked: Mass CMS Compromise Fuels Malware Distribution
threat_stories.narrative:
A coordinated campaign is exploiting critical flaws in Ghost CMS and WordPress plugins to mass-compromise web platforms and distribute ClickFix malware [2, 3, 4]. Attackers leverage CVE-2026-26980 in Ghost CMS to inject malicious scripts that hijack user sessions, while simultaneously weaponizing an unauthenticated admin creation flaw in the WP Maps Pro plugin to seize full control of WordPress sites [2, 4]. These compromised platforms serve as staging grounds for ClickFix campaigns, which manipulate user interactions to extract financial data and credentials [3, 4]. The scale of exploitation indicates automated scanning infrastructure, likely tied to the recently dismantled Dutch botnet [1].

The tactical execution is methodical. Automated scripts scan for exposed Ghost and WordPress instances, exploit the authentication bypass, and deploy ClickFix payloads that intercept user input and redirect traffic to malicious endpoints [3, 4]. The WP Maps Pro vulnerability requires zero authentication, allowing attackers to create rogue administrator accounts and modify site configurations instantly [2]. This eliminates the need for credential theft or social engineering, making exploitation trivial and highly scalable.

Web administrators cannot rely on application firewalls alone; these vulnerabilities bypass standard defenses by exploiting legitimate administrative endpoints. The convergence of CMS exploitation and ClickFix distribution creates a self-sustaining attack cycle: compromised sites generate revenue through malware redirection, funding further infrastructure and scanning operations. Immediate isolation and credential rotation are mandatory.

timeline.event: Automated scanning campaigns target exposed Ghost CMS and WordPress instances
timeline.significance: Initial reconnaissance and vulnerability validation across web infrastructure

timeline.event: Mass exploitation of CVE-2026-26980 and WP Maps Pro flaw begins
timeline.significance: Rogue admin accounts created and ClickFix payloads injected at scale

timeline.event: Public disclosure of active exploitation and Dutch botnet takedown
timeline.significance: Confirms infrastructure scale and forces immediate web platform remediation

threat_stories.impact_assessment: High. Thousands of web applications face immediate takeover, data theft, and use as malware distribution nodes. Financial and credential harvesting is actively occurring.
threat_stories.action_required: Disable WP Maps Pro and update Ghost CMS immediately. Deploy WAF rules to block unauthorized user creation endpoints and known ClickFix payload signatures. Audit all administrative accounts for unauthorized creations, rotate credentials, and restore clean backups where compromise is confirmed [2, 3, 4].

threat_stories.headline: Perimeter Erosion: VPN Authentication Bypassed via Cryptographic Misconfiguration
threat_stories.narrative:
Attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-0257 to bypass Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN authentication by forging session cookies [6]. The vulnerability exploits a common misconfiguration where the same certificate secures both the HTTPS service and cookie encryption. By extracting the public key from a legitimate HTTPS session, threat actors craft valid authentication cookies for any user, including local administrators, without requiring credentials [6]. Rapid7 confirmed active exploitation across multiple customer environments, proving that perimeter defenses are being circumvented through cryptographic trust assumptions rather than brute force [6].

The technical execution exploits the main_DecryptAppAuthCookie function, which decrypts incoming cookies but implicitly trusts the decrypted content without signature verification [6]. Attackers leverage this flaw to generate authenticated sessions that bypass all VPN gateway controls. This eliminates the security boundary of the perimeter, granting attackers direct network access and the ability to pivot into internal systems. The exploitation requires no user interaction and leaves minimal forensic artifacts, making detection exceptionally difficult.

Organizations that treat their VPN infrastructure as a secure boundary are operating under a false assumption. The GlobalProtect vulnerability proves that cryptographic misconfigurations can render perimeter controls completely ineffective. Security teams must assume their VPN gateways are compromised until proper certificate segregation is enforced and patch deployment is verified.

timeline.event: Attackers identify GlobalProtect deployments with shared HTTPS/cookie encryption certificates
timeline.significance: Target selection based on cryptographic misconfiguration patterns

timeline.event: Forged authentication cookies deployed to establish unauthorized VPN sessions
timeline.significance: Active perimeter bypass and internal network pivoting confirmed

timeline.event: Rapid7 publishes advisory and proof-of-concept script
timeline.significance: Confirms widespread exploitation and forces immediate cryptographic remediation

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Organizations using Palo Alto GlobalProtect with shared certificates face immediate unauthorized network access, potential domain compromise, and complete perimeter erosion.
threat_stories.action_required: Upgrade PAN-OS to the patched version immediately. Disable the authentication override feature. Generate a dedicated certificate exclusively for cookie encryption, ensuring it is never shared with the HTTPS service. Audit all recent GlobalProtect sessions for unauthorized access patterns [6].

actor_profiles.name: EKZ/ClickFix Distribution Network
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial theft, credential harvesting, malware-as-a-service distribution
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Poisoning FortiClient EMS update channels to deploy EKZ Infostealer, mass-compromising Ghost CMS instances to distribute ClickFix campaigns, and leveraging automated web exploitation scripts across thousands of targets [3, 4].

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Critical vulnerability allowing execution of malicious code disguised as legitimate Fortinet patches. Exploited to distribute EKZ Infostealer across managed endpoints.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Fortinet's latest security patch. Verify digital signatures of all EMS updates before installation. Disable automatic updates until verification is enforced.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authentication bypass enabling mass compromise of Ghost instances. Exploited to inject ClickFix malware and hijack user sessions across web platforms.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Ghost CMS to the latest patched version immediately. Deploy WAF rules to block ClickFix payloads. Restrict admin panel access via IP whitelisting.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authentication bypass allowing attackers to forge VPN session cookies when HTTPS and cookie encryption share the same certificate. Grants unauthorized network access.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade PAN-OS to patched version. Generate dedicated certificate for cookie encryption. Disable authentication override feature.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unauthenticated admin account creation vulnerability allowing full site takeover without credentials. Actively exploited to compromise WordPress installations.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable WP Maps Pro immediately. Update to patched version when available. Implement WAF rules blocking unauthorized user creation endpoints.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Remote code execution vulnerability allowing unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code on SharePoint servers. Actively targeted in the wild.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Install latest Microsoft security updates for SharePoint immediately. Restrict external access and implement network segmentation.

hunting_leads.title: FortiClient EMS Spoofed Patch Detection
hunting_leads.context: Attackers disguise EKZ Infostealer as legitimate Fortinet updates. Hunt for anomalous patch deployments bypassing signature verification.
hunting_leads.query: securityEvent where ProcessName contains "FortiClientEMS" and (FileName contains ".exe" or FileName contains ".msi") and not (DigitalSignatureVerified == true) | summarize count() by Hostname, FileName, Timestamp

hunting_leads.title: WordPress Unauthenticated Admin Creation
hunting_leads.context: WP Maps Pro vulnerability allows zero-authentication admin account creation. Monitor for sudden admin role assignments without login events.
hunting_leads.query: index=web_access wp_maps_pro* | where user_role == "administrator" and login_event == null | stats count by site_url, created_user, timestamp

hunting_leads.title: Palo Alto GlobalProtect Cookie Forgery
hunting_leads.context: CVE-2026-0257 allows forged VPN cookies. Hunt for authentication events lacking corresponding credential validation or showing anomalous session origins.
hunting_leads.query: CommonSecurityLog where Product == "GlobalProtect" and EventID == "authentication_success" and not (AuthenticationMethod == "certificate" or AuthenticationMethod == "2fa") | where CookieSignature != ExpectedSignature | summarize count() by SourceIP, Username, Timestamp

hunting_leads.title: Ghost CMS ClickFix Injection
hunting_leads.context: CVE-2026-26980 exploited to deploy ClickFix malware. Hunt for unauthorized script injections and session hijacking patterns in CMS logs.
hunting_leads.query: index=cms_access ghost* | where request_uri contains "/ghost/api" and (response_code == 200 or response_code == 302) and payload contains "clickfix" | stats count by site_domain, injected_script, timestamp

statistics.key_changes: Shift from opportunistic web scanning to targeted poisoning of enterprise update channels and cryptographic bypass of perimeter controls. Attackers now weaponize vendor trust mechanisms and misconfigured certificates to achieve persistent access.
emerging_trends: Weaponization of vendor update mechanisms for malware distribution
Cryptographic trust exploitation for perimeter bypass
AI developer tool supply chain targeting
Automated CMS exploitation for mass malware staging
